title: Sanitize Your CPR Pocket Mask
description: Clean and disinfect your CPR mask so it's safe for the next emergency.

dialogue.text:
Your CPR pocket mask hasn't been cleaned since the last practice session.
Let's sanitize it so it's ready for the next use.

options.text: How do I clean it safely?

dialogue.text: Before sanitizing, inspect the mask valve and straps for cracks, and set up three labeled containers: wash, disinfect, and rinse. Wear nitrile gloves and avoid mixing bleach with any cleaner except water.

options.text: Mask passes inspection and station is ready
I found damage or I cannot verify the bleach ratio

dialogue.text: Wash the mask in warm soapy water, rinse thoroughly, then soak in a fresh 1:10 bleach solution for 2 full minutes. Move it to clean rinse water, then place on a clean towel to air dry. Record the soak start/end time so contact time is provable.

options.text: Soak timed, rinsed, and drying on a clean surface
I dropped it or touched it with dirty hands

dialogue.text: Safety check: the mask must be fully dry with no bleach odor before storage. If moisture or odor remains, continue air drying and re-rinse.

options.text: Dry, odor-free, and resealed in the first aid kit
Still damp or strong bleach smell remains

dialogue.text: Do not sanitize a damaged mask. Quarantine and replace the mask before your next CPR practice so equipment failure does not delay care.
Contamination recovery: discard the dirty solution, wash your hands, prep a new bleach mix, and restart from the sanitize step.
All clean! A sanitized mask protects both you and your patient during resuscitation.
